title: Balustrade Coffee Table
summary: A coffee table on four purchased turned pillar legs, with a 2x4 apron under a planked 1x6 top and a lower 2x6 shelf frame raised on short baluster-like blocks.
description:
The four legs here are purchased turned pillars rather than cut lumber — this build is about the frame around them, not the legs themselves, so source your pillars before cutting anything else, since the apron height depends on the pillar length you land on. Build the 2x4 apron as a simple rectangular frame, cutting the two long members to fit your actual pillar spacing rather than trusting a fixed number.

The planked 1x6 top glues and screws to the apron from below, ten boards running the width of the table — keep the boards tight and let the plank lines show, since that's the intended look. Small 1x4 blocks under each pillar give the turned legs a proper mounting surface.

**The lower shelf is what gives this table its "balustrade" name** — a 2x6 frame raised on short 2x6 blocks standing on edge like stubby balusters between the main legs, creating a stepped, layered look underneath the tabletop rather than a plain flat shelf.

materials.name: Wood screws, 2" or longer, and nails for the feet
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: May need to be cut to fit your own pillar spacing.
May need to be cut to fit.

steps.title: Source the legs and build the apron
steps.body: Confirm your turned pillar leg length, then build the 2x4 apron frame to fit, cutting the long sides to your actual spacing.

steps.title: Mount the legs
steps.body: Attach the mounting blocks and the four turned pillar legs to the corners of the apron.
materials: Wood screws, 2" or longer, and nails for the feet

steps.title: Build the lower shelf
steps.body: Build the 2x6 shelf frame, then raise it on the baluster blocks between the main legs.

steps.title: Attach the shelf boards
steps.body: Attach the shelf boards across the raised shelf frame.
materials: Wood screws, 2" or longer, and nails for the feet

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: Glue and screw the ten planked top boards to the apron from below.
materials: Wood screws, 2" or longer, and nails for the feet

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Balustrade coffee table with turned pillar legs and lower shelf
